title: UNSW Researchers to Scale Up Development of Clean Aviation Fuel Thanks to $1.2M TRaCE Award
content: UNSW researchers are set to scale up a next-generation electrolyser system that helps produce sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) using renewable energy, thanks to a $1.2 million grant from the TRaCE Lab to Market Fund. The electrolyser converts carbon dioxide and water into precursors that can then ultimately be turned into green fuel for aircraft. The funding from TRaCE, the Trailblazer for Recycling and Clean Energy program established by the Australian Department of Education, has been boosted by industry partners to total $4.2 million, with the aim of achieving a critical breakthrough in the reduction of emissions in aviation.
author: Analysts Account
